task: Run mix maestro.refactor_to_daisyui on the Maestro app itself
context: The refactor tool is now fully AST-based (no regex). It uses MaestroTool.HEExParser to parse HEEx, traverse the AST, transform class attributes, and render back. Both DaisyuiRefactor (component replacement) and CssExtractor (utility extraction) are ready. Time to eat our own cooking.
steps: Run `mix maestro.refactor_to_daisyui` (dry run) to see what raw Tailwind patterns exist in Maestro's own templates
Review the analysis — decide which patterns to refactor vs. skip
Run `mix maestro.refactor_to_daisyui --apply` to apply component refactoring (buttons → btn, cards → card, etc.)
Run `mix maestro.refactor_to_daisyui --extract-css` to identify repeated utility patterns that should be semantic CSS classes
If extraction looks good, run `mix maestro.refactor_to_daisyui --extract-css --apply --css-file assets/css/maestro-components.css` to generate the CSS file and rewrite templates
Add `@import "./maestro-components.css";` to app.css if the file was generated
Verify the app compiles and renders correctly
Review changes with `git diff` — fix any issues the tool missed or got wrong
Report back what worked, what didn't, and what patterns the tool should handle better
engine_modules: /Users/vince/dev/forks/maestro_tool/lib/maestro/daisyui_refactor.ex — component refactoring (btn, card, badge, footer, navbar, rating)
key_principle: This is the tool testing itself on a real codebase. Any issues found should be fixed IN THE TOOL, not manually in the templates. Report gaps so the tool improves.

expected_patterns.buttons: Any <a> or <button> with px-* py-* bg-* rounded-full font-bold → btn btn-primary/secondary
expected_patterns.cards: divs with rounded-md overflow-hidden → card + card-body + card-title
expected_patterns.badges: spans with text-xs rounded-full font-bold uppercase → badge
expected_patterns.star_ratings: div with flex gap-* containing hero-star-solid icons → rating
expected_patterns.css_extraction: repeated utility combos (3+ utils, 2+ occurrences) → semantic CSS classes
